input.background:
Suppose that $\mathrm{X}_1, \mathrm{X}_2, \ldots, \mathrm{X}_{\mathrm{n}}$ are independent random variables, each with the following probability density function:

$$
f(x, \alpha, \beta)= \begin{cases}0 & \text { if } x<\alpha \\ \frac{1}{\beta} e^{-\frac{(x-\alpha)}{\beta}} & \text { if } x \geq \alpha\end{cases}
$$

where $-\infty<\alpha<\infty$ and $0<\beta<\infty$ are both unknown.
input.question: (a) Find the maximum likelihood estimators (MLE) of $\alpha$ and $\beta$, say $\hat{\alpha}$ and $\hat{\beta}$, respectively.

output.answer: The MLE of $\alpha$ and $\beta$ are derived based on the likelihood function.
